steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The second sentence claims that Montaigne questions his own ideas “no matter the essay topic.” The third sentence repeats this exact claim using different wording: he “questions his own perspective, regardless of subject matter.” This is a confirmation pattern—the second statement reinforces the first's central point.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
”Indeed” is a confirmation transition used when a statement reinforces what was just said.

The third sentence reinforces the claim from the second sentence by restating Montaigne's approach to questioning himself.

The other choices don't fit: “For example” introduces a specific case, “At that time” implies a time shift, and “However” signals contradiction.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
